task_completion.description: Investigate local user accounts with failed logon attempts in ADAudit Plus.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the ADAudit Plus web interface displaying a 'Local Logon Failures' or similar report, with 'bruteforce1' visible as having the highest number of failed attempts.

task_completion.description: Investigate file access events in monitored audit folders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the ADAudit Plus web interface displaying a 'File Audit' or 'File Access' report, identifying the account (e.g., Docker or SYSTEM) that accessed sensitive files.

task_completion.description: Create the 'security_ops' technician account with the 'Operator' role.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the ADAudit Plus Admin/Delegation settings showing a technician account named 'security_ops' created with the role 'Operator' and email 'secops@corp.local'.

task_completion.description: Set up an email notification profile with threshold-based alerting for failed logons.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the ADAudit Plus Alert Profiles configuration showing an alert set for failed logons with a threshold of more than 5 events within 10 minutes.
Screenshots of the ADAudit Plus Scheduled Reports section showing a report named 'Security Summary' configured with a 'Daily' schedule.

task_completion.description: Document findings in a comprehensive threat assessment report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a text editor (e.g., Notepad) open with the file 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\threat_assessment.txt'. The content must include the identified threat accounts (e.g., 'bruteforce1'), threat classifications, and prioritized remediation steps.

integrity.description: The agent used the ADAudit Plus web GUI to perform investigations and configurations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the ADAudit Plus web interface in a browser (http://localhost:8081/), rather than just executing API calls or PowerShell scripts in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent actually viewed the reports before writing the assessment.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be screenshots of the agent actively looking at the logon failure and file access reports in ADAudit Plus, not just guessing the accounts or hallucinating the findings.

integrity.description: The agent manually created or properly saved the threat assessment text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent using a text editor (like Notepad) or a standard file writing method to compose the threat assessment, rather than just copying a pre-made script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The primary threat account with the highest failed logons is 'bruteforce1' (25 attempts). Secondary threats are 'testattacker' and 'wrongadmin'. The file access events were generated by the system account (e.g., Docker/SYSTEM), not 'jsmith'. The technician account must be 'security_ops' with the 'Operator' role. The scheduled report must be named 'Security Summary' and run daily. The final report must be saved to 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\threat_assessment.txt'.
